title: All on one line
checkLabels.t3: The heading is the cafe name
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted everything instead of adding tags. Put the code back.
why.not-closed: Some tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: Look at the last tag you added. Every opening tag has a twin with a slash.
why.no-h1: There is no main heading. The cafe name is still bare text.
hint2.no-h1: Wrap the first line in body in a first-level heading tag.
why.h1-wrong-text: There is a heading, but the cafe name is not inside it.
hint2.h1-wrong-text: The heading must contain Bean Street Coffee, exactly as in the starting text.
why.many-h1: There is more than one first-level heading.
hint2.many-h1: A page has one main heading. It says what the WHOLE page is, not what one of its lines is.
why.no-p: There is no paragraph. The sentence under the name is still bare text.
hint2.no-p: The second line is a sentence. A sentence is a paragraph.
why.p-wrong-text: There is a paragraph, but the sentence from the starting text is not inside it.
hint2.p-wrong-text: The paragraph must contain Coffee worth the second visit.
why.lost-menu: The coffee lines are gone. You deleted them.
hint2.lost-menu: A tag goes AROUND the text, not instead of it. Put the three priced lines back — they are the next lesson's job.
blocks.0.text: The owner of Bean Street sent the menu in an email. You put it in the page exactly as it arrived: name, sentence, three coffees with prices. Each on its own line.
blocks.1.text: Look at the preview on the right. Everything is glued into one line. You did nothing wrong — the file looks exactly the way you wrote it.
blocks.3.text: The browser does not read whitespace. A new line, two spaces, a tab — to it they are the same thing: a separator between two words. It makes no difference how many times you pressed Enter.
blocks.5.text: To the browser these two lines are four words and two numbers, one after another. Nothing in them says they are separate things. You know it. It does not.
blocks.6.text: Whitespace is not structure. Structure is written.
blocks.8.text: Make the first two lines real. The cafe name is the main heading of the page. The sentence below it is a paragraph.
blocks.9.text: Leave the three coffees as they are. They are a list, and the list is the next lesson.
